TextENGNew: Fatigue and a headache.
I woke up... feeling terrible.
``What's wrong?@k@vS01/00/122700012. Keiichi.@k@vS01/00/122700013. ...You look pale this morning.@k@vS01/00/122700014. What time did you go to sleep?``
Mom seemed to have noticed how wretched I looked.
...Last night... I had woken up over and over in the dead of night.
It was unmistakable—a presence.
...The presence of someone standing right outside my bedroom door.
I told myself again and again that it was just my imagination, struggling to fall back asleep.
But... eventually I couldn't take it anymore, and I would summon what courage I had... and open the door.
...Of course, no one was ever there.
I did this at least three times.
...Or perhaps more—I may simply not have remembered the rest.
I couldn't help but feel relieved that I'd made it through the night unscathed, safely seated at the breakfast table.
changeReason: Old says 'I still didn't feel at ease' (negative) which is the opposite of JPN 安堵を覚えずにはいられなかった (couldn't help but feel relieved).

TextENGNew: ``I think I might have a cold.@k@vS01/01/120100272. ...I just don't have any appetite.``
``...Oh my.@k@vS01/00/122700016. ...You do feel a little warm...@k@vS01/00/122700017. What do you want to do?@k@vS01/00/122700018. Can you make it to school?``
School was mostly self-study anyway.@k ...Missing one day wouldn't really matter.
Maybe... the reason I'd been feeling so off these past few days really was just a cold.
If I took some medicine and rested for one good day, maybe I'd be able to greet everyone with a smile tomorrow.
Ding dong...!
I flinched and looked back and forth between the door and the clock.
...It was already more than ten minutes past the time I usually met Rena.@k ...It had to be Rena.
``Rena-chan's here to pick you up... what do you want to do?@k@vS01/00/122700020. Are you staying home?``
...Rena was a good person.@k She could be a little eccentric, sure, but she was sweet.@k Her lunches were delicious, and she always looked out for me.
...So why was I so afraid of her...?
Rena wasn't to blame.
If anything, I was the one at fault...
It had to be the cold.@k ...It just had to be.
``I'll stay home.@k@vS01/01/120100274. ...Sorry.``
``Then I'll go tell her for you.``
Mom headed for the front door.
...To get back to my room, I'd have to pass by the entrance.
I couldn't face Rena... so I wrapped myself in a blanket on the sofa and closed my eyes.
Between the sleep deprivation and exhaustion, I sank into a deep slumber almost immediately...
I'd only meant to lie down for a bit, but when I opened my eyes, it was already almost noon.
I called out, but my parents didn't seem to be home. On the dining table, I found lunch prepared and a note.
Apparently, Mom and Dad had driven off somewhere far away.
Probably something to do with Dad's work.
It happened from time to time—nothing unusual.
They'd be back by dinner, though they might be a little late.
The insurance card is in the dresser, so take it and go to the hospital.
changeReason: Old contains the note's introduction 'On the note, it said:' while new contains the actual note content about the insurance card; content was misaligned between this entry and the next in the original translation.

TextENGNew: ...That's what the note said.
changeReason: Old contains the note's content about the insurance card while new contains 'That's what the note said'; content was swapped with the previous entry -- the new version restores correct ordering matching the JPN.

TextENGNew: Mom had even drawn a careful map to the clinic.
changeReason: Changes 'hospital' to 'clinic'; the JPN refers to Irie's 診療所 (clinic), not a hospital, matching the consistent in-game terminology.

TextENGNew: ...Right.@k I'd never been there, so I didn't really know where it was.
I picked at lunch—really just the morning's leftovers—then decided to go to the clinic, just in case.
changeReason: Changes 'hospital' to 'clinic' matching the in-game terminology for Irie's small diagnostic clinic.

TextENGNew: Being out in the middle of a weekday, when I'd normally never be—@k ...just walking around made me feel guilty. It was a strange feeling.
Following Mom's map, I turned onto a road I'd never used before. After walking for a while, the clinic's sign came into view.
changeReason: Changes 'hospital' to 'clinic' matching the consistent in-game terminology for Irie's establishment.

TextENGNew: The sign read 'Irie Clinic,' written in fairly elegant lettering.
The clinic wasn't large by any means, but for a place the size of Hinamizawa, it was impressively well-appointed.
It even had a proper parking lot with a dedicated space for buses.@k ...Business must have been good.
I sat spacing out in the cool, air-conditioned waiting room until it was my turn...
``Maebara-san, the doctor will see you now.``
After half-heartedly answering the chatty doctor's battery of questions, he gave me the standard diagnosis: 'It's a cold.'
I got a shot and three days' worth of medicine.
...It seemed like a bit much, but if it could shake the gloom that had been hanging over me, it was worth it.
I paid up and was about to leave after a quick trip to the restroom when I overheard a conversation among some elderly folks who were clearly regulars.
Of course, I had no intention of paying attention.@k ...If not for that one word.
``...Must've been an Onikakushi.``
``Who knows.@k@vS01/00/villB02002. She was still pretty young.@k@vS01/00/villB02003. Might've just run off with someone.``
``Run off with who?``
``That city fellow who came around every season.@k@vS01/00/villB02005. The young one with the great big camera.@k@vS01/00/villB02006. You didn't know they were close?``
...Were they talking about Tomitake-san?!
I could feel my ears perk up.
``I wouldn't know about that...@k@vS01/00/villA02003. But... even if you elope, you've got obligations.@k@vS01/00/villA02004. You'd leave a note, or at least quit your job first.``
``That's what makes it an elopement—she didn't leave a thing.``
``...It'll be sad not seeing that young nurse's face around here anymore...``
``Takano-san is a capable woman.@k@vS01/00/villA02006. ...She'll manage wherever she ends up.``
Her name was Takano-san?@k ...The woman who was with Tomitake-san.@k And... she worked at this very clinic...!?
Once again, Oyashiro-sama's shadow reached out and touched my life.
I listened for a while longer, but the conversation drifted to fishing and showed no sign of returning. I gave up and left.
Even when I escaped the routine of school, I couldn't outrun Oyashiro-sama's shadow.
...Of course not.
Oyashiro-sama was the guardian deity, after all.@k ...Of this place—Hinamizawa.
As long as I remained in Hinamizawa, there was no escape...
Maybe it was the walk in the fresh air, but the appetite I'd lost that morning came rushing back.
I could use the change from the doctor's visit to grab some bread on the way home.
With that thought, I turned onto a familiar road.
That was when a car horn suddenly blared from behind me.
...Was I really walking so far in the middle of the road that I was in the way?
I moved closer to the shoulder, but the horn honked again. Annoyed, I turned around.
``Maebara-sa~n.@k@vS01/11/120700193. Good afternoon.``
It was Ooishi-san.@k He was leaning out of his car, AC cranked to full blast, waving broadly.
``What brings you out today?@k@vS01/11/120700195. No school?``
``I-I wasn't feeling well, so I stayed home today.@k@vS01/01/120100276. ...I was just about to buy some lunch and head back.``
``Perfect timing.@k@vS01/11/120700197. I was just heading out for lunch myself. Care to join me?@k@vS01/11/120700198. ...Ah, if you're not feeling up to it, don't worry about it.``
I was just going to lie around when I got home anyway.
...And it wasn't really a cold.
``You probably wouldn't want to eat at a local place.@k@vS01/11/120700200. Let's go into town.@k@vS01/11/120700201. It's a bit of a drive—is that all right?@k@vS01/11/120700202. I know a good spot.``
I was finally starting to understand Ooishi-san's roundabout way of putting things.
...He probably wanted to discuss something that would be hard to talk about in Hinamizawa.
I climbed into his ice-cold, air-conditioned car.
The blazing sun outside felt like a lie.
``Sorry again about calling so late last night.@k@vS01/11/120700204. Did your family get upset?``
I thought of Rena—who had been standing just on the other side of the door, listening to that entire phone call.
...Even now, in broad daylight, the thought made my skin crawl.
With a jarring bump, the gravel road leading into town gave way to pavement.
I gasped and swiveled to look behind us.@k ...That's right.@k This was where Tomitake-san had died.
``...The investigation into Tomitake-san has hit a dead end.``
changeReason: 'Hit a snag' vs 'hit a dead end'; JPN 暗礁に乗り上げています (run aground on a reef) suggests a more severe impasse than a temporary snag.

TextENGNew: Ooishi-san spoke while glancing at me from the corner of his eye.

``Given how he clawed out his own throat, we believed some kind of hallucinogenic drug was involved.@k@vS01/11/120700207. ...But apparently, no known substance can trigger symptoms like that so rapidly.``
``The autopsy was conducted with that possibility in mind, of course... but it yielded no leads.``
``Um... you know how in manga and things, there are drugs that break down inside the body and leave no trace?@k@vS01/01/120100279. Something like that...``

``Those do exist.@k@vS01/11/120700210. Succinylcholi—what was it again...@k@vS01/11/120700211. Well, yes, there are quite a few.@k@vS01/11/120700212. But the thing is, none of the drugs that could vanish without a trace are known to cause symptoms like Tomitake-san's.``
``...Then... are you saying the police have concluded that Tomitake-san died from the curse?!``
It wasn't that I was frustrated with the police's incompetence.
...I just desperately wanted to strip the word 'curse' away from Tomitake-san's death.

``As much as it pains me, that's how it stands at the moment.@k@vS01/11/120700214. But regardless of the cause of death, there's no doubt he was surrounded and beaten by several people before he died.@k@vS01/11/120700215. ...This was no curse. Human beings were unquestionably involved.``
That... was a small relief.
Eventually, we reached a rather run-down (but still livelier than Hinamizawa) area in front of the train station.
We pulled into a restaurant parking lot, and I followed Ooishi-san inside.
It was bustling, but at this hour, the clientele was entirely adults. Of course—no child would be out here in the middle of a weekday.
``Table for two, please. I'll survive without smoking.``
``Right this way, please.@k@vS01/00/waitA2002. When you're ready to order, just call me over.``
A waitress in a rather cute outfit showed us to our table. I sat down across from Ooishi-san in a booth.
``So, what do you think?@k@vS01/11/120700218. Don't you think the waitresses' uniforms here are cute?``
``Huh?@k@vS01/01/120100282. Well... I mean... I guess??``
``...This place is always packed.@k@vS01/11/120700220. More for the waitresses than the food, if you know what I mean~``
``Uh huh.@k@vS01/01/120100284. ...Ooishi-san, your eyes are wandering.``
``Oh?@k@vS01/11/120700222. Am I that obvious?@k@vS01/11/120700223. Heh heh heh heh...!``
...Waitress uniforms aside, the food was decent enough, I supposed...?
If we were going into town anyway, Angel Mort would have been better, but... that place was run by Mion's relatives.
I couldn't very well go somewhere I might run into people I knew while in the company of Ooishi-san...
We finished our meal while making idle conversation, and the after-dinner coffee Ooishi-san had ordered arrived.
``We talked about Hinamizawa's old legends yesterday, didn't we?``
``Yeah. You said the villagers were once called 'man-eating demons' or something like that.``
``I don't know all that much about it myself, so I asked my mother about it this morning.``
Ooishi-san pulled a folded memo from his breast pocket.
``It turns out that a long time ago, Hinamizawa was called 'Onigafuchi'—the Demon's Abyss.``
``Onigafuchi...@k@vS01/01/120100287. ...That's quite a name...``

``The name still exists today.@k@vS01/11/120700228. ...The swamp where the Shinto priest's wife drowned herself is still called Onigafuchi.@k@vS01/11/120700229. ...It was said that the very bottom of the swamp connected to the land where the demons dwelled.``
Of course, nobody had told me about such an eerie swamp...
``And you see,@k@vS01/11/120700231. Onigafuchi was feared and revered in equal measure.``
Feared and revered.@k ...A kind of sacred awe, perhaps.
A village where inhuman beings dwelled.

``The demons were also something like sages or mountain mystics, apparently.@k@vS01/11/120700233. For instance, if a villager came down with an incurable illness, they would be carried to Onigafuchi and the demons would heal them.``
Even calling them 'demons'... the image was more like tengu or hermit sages from folklore.
``They don't sound like such terrible beings, even if they were called demons.``

``But they were still man-eaters at the end of the day.@k@vS01/11/120700235. ...According to the tale my mother told me, when a mother brought her sick son to be healed, the demons' price was: 'Let us devour the mother who brought him!'``
``...The price of curing her son... was her own life?!@k@vS01/01/120100290. ...That's terrifying.``
``Of course, the mother grabbed her son and ran.@k@vS01/11/120700237. And then, you see, all the villagers of Onigafuchi—the demons—chased them down together, surrounded them, and captured them.``
The entire village, all at once.
...That was a truly horrifying scene to imagine.
``In the end, both mother and son were caught... and eaten.@k@vS01/11/120700239. The end.``
``There's a contradiction in that story.@k@vS01/01/120100292. ...If both of them were eaten, there'd be no one left to tell the tale.``
``Oh?@k@vS01/11/120700241. ...Ha ha ha ha! Old folk tales are full of those kinds of holes.``
In stark contrast to the gruesome tale, Ooishi-san laughed heartily as he sipped his coffee.
``But here's the thing.@k@vS01/11/120700243. ...It appears in quite a few other legends as well.@k@vS01/11/120700244. The demons of Onigafuchi banding together to hunt down their prey.``
I suddenly recalled Mion's words about how the entire village had united to fight the dam construction.
...That part about acting as one... it overlapped just a little too neatly.
``The demons' prey was always a single person, and the target was decided in advance.``
That was something I couldn't let pass.
...Ooishi-san seemed to feel the same, his expression taking on a meaningful weight.
``My mother told me that during the 'demon's hunt,' you must never interfere.@k@vS01/11/120700247. You close yourself up in your house and hide under the covers, she said.``
``...So what does that mean, exactly?``

``It means you must never help or shelter the prey.@k@vS01/11/120700249. As long as you don't interfere with the demon's hunt, no harm will come to the villagers.@k@vS01/11/120700250. Those were the rules, apparently.``
In short: don't help the victim.
Turn a blind eye to the atrocity... is what they meant.
``What happens if you break the rules?``
``Well now...@k@vS01/11/120700252. ...They are man-eating demons, after all.``
So much of that story had caught my attention.
...Because parts of it overlapped with Ooishi-san's theory that the whole village was complicit.

Even if the culprit wasn't the entire village, it was entirely plausible that the villagers had 'hidden under their covers and looked the other way' while a few perpetrators carried out their 'hunt'... wasn't it???
The mere thought was terrifying.
``...So, Ooishi-san... you believe it's either the entire village, or a group of villagers behind these crimes...?``
``What do you think, Maebara-san?@k@vS01/11/120700254. ...Oh, excuse me.@k@vS01/11/120700255. Could I get a refill on the coffee?``
I was the one asking here.
...Or perhaps we were both just trying to foist the final, unspeakable conclusion onto the other...
Neither of us spoke while the waitress refilled his coffee.
``...Last year, when Satoshi-kun disappeared, I started to feel that something was off.``
Ooishi-san murmured, watching the milk swirl in his cup.

``So I did a bit of checking—just a little, mind you—@k@vS01/11/120700258. Really, just a tiny bit, okay?@k@vS01/11/120700259. —into Satoshi-san's friends. That is to say, your friend group, Maebara-san.``
If he'd said this to me before, I would have snapped at him for suspecting my friends.@k ...But this time, I didn't.
``What I'm about to tell you may seem quite dull.@k@vS01/11/120700261. ...If at any point you feel it's meaningless, Maebara-san, just say so.@k@vS01/11/120700262. I'll stop.``
Ooishi-san wore the most serious expression I had ever seen from him.
...He was telling me to steel myself and listen.
There were many things Ooishi-san had told me that I wished I could unhear.
...But none had come with a warning this grave...
A voice inside me sounded the alarm...!
Stop it, Keiichi.@k This is probably... the point of no return...!
I drew a deep breath and silenced that voice.
...I would not run from the truth.
``...Please, go ahead.``
That was all I could manage to say.
...Ooishi-san held his silence for a long moment, staring into my eyes—confirming my resolve—before he began.
``The victim of the first year's incident was the site foreman. In the weeks before the crime, he had physical altercations with Mion Sonozaki-san.@k@vS01/11/120700264. On multiple occasions.``
...Ooishi-san had told me before that Mion had resisted the dam project aggressively.
...Well, knowing Mion, it wasn't hard to imagine her getting heated.
``In the second year, the pro-dam couple died in that accident, right?@k@vS01/11/120700266. Their daughter was with them at the scene.@k@vS01/11/120700267. ...That daughter is Satoko Houjou-san.``
``What?!@k@vS01/01/120100299. Satoko, as in... Satoko?!?!``
Ooishi-san silenced me with a look that said I was being too loud.@k ...I caught myself and lowered my voice.
``The Shinto priest and his wife who died in the third year.@k@vS01/11/120700269. Their daughter is Rika Furude-san.``

``The housewife who was killed in the fourth year—you already know, don't you?@k@vS01/11/120700271. She was Satoko Houjou-san's step-aunt.@k@vS01/11/120700272. ...By that point, Satoko-san had lost both parents in the accident, so she'd been placed in her aunt and uncle's care.@k@vS01/11/120700273. She was living in the victim's household.``
I could feel my lips going dry...
I... had even forgotten to close my slackened jaw.
I'd thought of these incidents as bizarre—yet somehow distant, having nothing to do with me.
...But now, piece by piece, clicking into place... they were assembling themselves right at my feet...
``By the way... the one who vanished in the fourth year, Satoshi Houjou-san, was Satoko-san's biological older brother.``
``Wha... just... wait... hold on a second...``
That was all I could manage to force out.
...I drained my glass of water in one gulp and scrubbed my face hard with the wet towel...
Calm down, Maebara Keiichi...!
But Ooishi-san was merciless.
...Without waiting for me to collect myself, he delivered the final blow.
The one thing... I should never have heard...
``For some reason, every single victim connects back to your group of friends.``
``...That has to be a coincidence!!``
``Maebara-san, please, keep it down...@k@vS01/11/120700277. People are staring...``
Mion...?@k Satoko?!@k Rika-chan?!
So what if they're connected?!
My friends—the whole group—are you saying...?!?!
That can't be... there's no way...@k ...Wait—Rena is different!
Rena has no connection to any of the victims.
``Rena Ryuugu-san was living in a suburb in Ibaraki Prefecture until last year.@k@vS01/11/120700279. It's true that she had no direct acquaintance with any of the victims, but...``
He trailed off without committing to anything.
Was he saying she was connected too...?!

``When I looked into her, I found that Ryuugu-san had been suspended from her previous school shortly before moving to Hinamizawa.@k@vS01/11/120700281. Apparently, she went around smashing every window in the building.``
``R-Rena did that?!@k@vS01/01/120100304. ...She smashed every window in the school?!?!``
That... spacey, dreamy... Rena...?!
I couldn't even begin to imagine it.

``It seems she didn't return to school even after her three-day suspension was over.@k@vS01/11/120700283. During that time, she was visiting a psychiatrist... and received several weeks of medication and counseling.``
Something like a nervous breakdown, perhaps.
...I'd heard that sort of thing happened to people who were very methodical or high-strung.
...Neither of those descriptions matched the carefree Rena I knew...
``...And in the medical notes from those counseling sessions, the doctor recorded their conversations...@k@vS01/11/120700285. A certain word appears in them.@k@vS01/11/120700286. Quite frequently.``
``What word...?``
I pressed him without thinking.
...What more could there possibly be to regret...?
``It appears...@k@|@y@vS01/11/120700288. the word 'Oyashiro-sama.'``
I felt as though an ice-cold hand had stroked my spine...
...Why... had Rena been talking about Oyashiro-sama before she even came to Hinamizawa...?!
``She said this thing—this ghost-like being called Oyashiro-sama—would come to her house night after night.@k@vS01/11/120700290. Standing at her bedside, looking down at her.``
...My thoughts froze solid.
...I couldn't comprehend... what I was being told...
``Not long after that, she moved to Hinamizawa.@k@vS01/11/120700292. ...Oh, and I should mention.@k@vS01/11/120700293. Rena-san isn't actually an outsider at all.``
``According to the residency records, the Ryuugu family originally lived in Hinamizawa.@k@vS01/11/120700295. They moved to Ibaraki right when Rena-san was starting elementary school.``
My mind went blank—filled with nothing but white static...
...It was like the snow on a television screen after the broadcast had ended late at night...
A high-pitched ringing filled my ears, and I lost all grasp on reality...
``...Maebara-san, are you all right?@k@vS01/11/120700297. Should we stop?``
Those words jolted me back.@k ...No—I couldn't let it end here...!
``...I understand about Rena now...@k@vS01/01/120100308. Then what about... the latest victim, Tomitake-san?@k@vS01/01/120100309. ...Who was he connected to...?``
It was my last, desperate counterattack.

``...All of them.@k@vS01/11/120700299. Have you forgotten, Maebara-san?@k@vS01/11/120700300. Didn't you all spend the night of the festival together, enjoying yourselves?@k@vS01/11/120700301. Quite a few officers saw your merry group.``
...There was nothing left I could say...
...I hung my head in silence...
``Shall we get going?@k@vS01/11/120700303. Oh, Maebara-san—did you take your afternoon medicine?``
I'd completely forgotten about the medicine I'd gotten today until Ooishi-san reminded me...
I asked for another glass of water, downed it along with the pills, and we left.
We got in the car and bumped our way back to Hinamizawa along the rough road.
...I'd never paid much attention on my bicycle, but did cars really shake this much on this road...?
...It felt as if the road itself were thrashing about, desperately trying to tell me something.
*Thud!*
...That violent jolt was from the drop where pavement gave way to gravel.
I could have sworn I heard Tomitake-san's silent scream...
I sat without saying a word, just letting the car jostle me around...
``I'll drop you off at your doorstep.@k@vS01/11/120700305. ...You stayed home sick today, after all.@k@vS01/11/120700306. I'm truly sorry for burdening you with such unpleasant talk.``
``...Why are you telling me all this?``
The words came out in a low mutter.
...A bare whisper, really.@k I hadn't expected an answer.
``I did check with you first, didn't I?@k@vS01/11/120700308. Whether we should stop.``
``That's not what I mean!@k@vS01/01/120100312. ...Why did you approach me in the first place?``
...I understood perfectly well that Ooishi-san was investigating the connections between these suspicious incidents.
But... why was he sharing all of it with me...?
I knew nothing and could offer nothing.@k Everything he'd told me was entirely new information.
...In the first place, what could someone who'd just moved here possibly know?
If... there was any reason for Ooishi-san to seek me out...
...it was because I was one of Mion's circle—the group he considered suspicious.
``...I'm retiring this year, you see.@k@vS01/11/120700310. My mother wants to move away once I do.@k@vS01/11/120700311. ...That's why I want to bring the truth to light while I'm still on the job.``
``...So... you do suspect them, Ooishi-san.@k@vS01/01/120100314. All of them.``
Ooishi-san didn't reply.
A little late for that, I thought—but maybe it was his way of showing whatever conscience he had left.
``This is... the intuition of thirty years in this line of work.@k@vS01/11/120700313. ...Maebara-san. You're the one who's in danger.``
I wanted to snap back with 'That's absurd!'—but in my current state, I didn't have the energy.
``I'm retiring this year, so I won't be here for next year's Watanagashi.@k@vS01/11/120700315. ...That's why I need to settle this before then.``
He was implying, in no uncertain terms, that I might be next year's victim...

``The chief has warned me off, too.@k@vS01/11/120700317. ...He says each incident has already been resolved individually, so I should stop digging them up.@k@vS01/11/120700318. ...This is the kind of pressure I'm dealing with.``
``Pressure... from whom?``
``Someone in Hinamizawa.@k@vS01/11/120700320. ...We've arrived.@k@vS01/11/120700321. Will here do?``
Before I knew it, we'd pulled up to the slope leading to my house.@k The clock read 2:00.@k ...I was stunned at how little time had passed since we'd left the restaurant.
The heat outside the car was stifling.@k The shrill chorus of cicadas stung my ears.
``You're welcome to forget everything I told you today.@k@vS01/11/120700323. ...But I intend to keep investigating.@k@vS01/11/120700324. I will put an end to Oyashiro-sama's curse this year.``
``...So you're saying I should contact you if anything happens?``
``It's fine.@k@vS01/11/120700326. ...Just contact me when—and only when—you feel you absolutely have to, Maebara-san.``
I wasn't calm enough to decipher Ooishi-san's careful phrasing.
``Get plenty of rest.@k@vS01/11/120700328. ...I'm sorry for piling all this on you while you're home sick.``
I didn't bother to respond.
``I'm on your side. Always.@k@vS01/11/120700330. ...Believe that, at least.@k@vS01/11/120700331. Well then, take care.``
The tires crunched over the gravel as the car made a U-turn and vanished into a cloud of dust.
It was like watching a boat toss me a single life ring into shark-infested waters before disappearing beyond the buoys.
...For the first time, I thought Ooishi-san was a coward.
He'd told me I was going to be the next victim—then told me to call if anything happened.
...This wasn't a police investigation.
This was just fishing.
...And I was nothing but chum.
Would the fish he caught be the culprit?@k Or would it truly be Oyashiro-sama's curse?
...Either way, the bait would end up swallowed whole...
``...Damn it... I... don't want to die...``
For a long time, all I could do was stare at the puddle of condensation left behind by the car's air conditioner...
